free_text: Production of residual nuclides by proton-induced
reactions on target W at an energy of 72 MeV
The target in a stack consisted of three thin foils of
natural tungsten of high purity (99,99%) with diameters
of 15 mm and a thickness of 0,050 mm.
The foils in stacks were arranged in such a way
that Al and Cu-foils were placed in front and behind of
The contribution due to the fluctuation of
proton flux and the secondary particles produced in the
target stack was taken in account and found to be
The irradiation of target stacks was performed
The distances between the sample and detector
were varied to keep the dead time below 10%.
Contamination due to recoil   not considered
All the uncertainties were added in quadrature.
